title: Anesthesia procedure note documentation prompt
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.
assessment.rationale: The exact ASA section supports an anesthesia procedure record containing staff, monitoring, technique, medicines, positioning, lines, unusual events, fluids, output, and transfer status as observed facts; the generic legacy fields do not capture most of these domains.

source.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
source.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
source.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
source.exclusions: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.

assessment.text: Supports complete, contemporaneous records for assessment, consent, anesthesia or sedation, procedure, monitoring, recovery, discharge, complications, and follow-up.
assessment.rationale: The UAE record section was reviewed for complete procedure, anesthesia or sedation, monitoring, recovery, complication, and follow-up records, but no additional specific legacy item was available to map.

source.title: Standard for Day Surgery/Procedure Service
source.population: Patients receiving planned day surgery or procedure services in DoH-licensed facilities, with age, risk, procedure, anesthesia, and discharge qualifications.
source.setting: DoH-licensed day surgery and procedure facilities in Abu Dhabi.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for service governance, assessment, care plans, pre/intra/post-procedure documentation, records, discharge, and patient education. It does not replace anesthesiologist judgment, facility policy, consent, credentialing, or emergency care.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports complete, contemporaneous records for assessment, consent, anesthesia or sedation, procedure, monitoring, recovery, discharge, complications, and follow-up.

assessment.text: Supports documented procedure name, counts, specimen labeling, equipment problems, and key recovery or management concerns without generating a plan.
assessment.rationale: The WHO sign-out section was reviewed for procedure name, counts, specimens, equipment issues, and recovery concerns but was not mapped to the generic legacy scaffold.

source.title: WHO Guidelines for Safe Surgery 2009: Safe Surgery Saves Lives — Surgical Safety Checklist and Implementation Manual
source.population: Patients undergoing surgery or invasive procedures where the checklist is applied.
source.setting: Operating room or procedural setting before anesthesia, before incision, and before leaving the room.
source.exclusions: Exact for checklist documentation of identity, site, procedure, consent, anesthesia equipment checks, pulse oximetry, allergy, airway or aspiration risk, blood loss, team communication, procedure record, counts, specimens, equipment issues, and recovery concerns. It does not replace local UAE policy.

exact_location.heading: Before patient leaves operating room — Sign Out

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documented procedure name, counts, specimen labeling, equipment problems, and key recovery or management concerns without generating a plan.

sources_used.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
WHO Guidelines for Safe Surgery 2009: Safe Surgery Saves Lives — Surgical Safety Checklist and Implementation Manual

curation_policy: Direct full-source guideline reconstruction; every legacy item has an auditable comparison and every final clinical item retains exact source provenance.
